entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Owner of the workout tracking system, 33 years old

entities.name: Engine Building
entities.context: 80/20 running archetype — the 80% easy run focused on foundation, mitochondria, and base building in Zone 2

entities.name: The 20% Interval
entities.context: 80/20 running archetype — the 20% hard run focused on 4x4 intervals, VO2 max, and explosive power in Zone 4/5

entities.name: Heart Rate Zone Model
entities.context: 5-zone HR model (Z1–Z5) calibrated for age 33, with Z2 ceiling at 131 bpm and max HR ~220 bpm

entities.name: 80/20 Running
entities.context: Training philosophy splitting volume into 80% easy (Z2) and 20% hard (Z4/Z5) sessions

entities.name: Zone 2 Training
entities.context: HR range 113–131 bpm targeting aerobic base, mitochondrial density, and fat oxidation

entities.name: VO2 Max Training
entities.context: High-intensity intervals in Z4/Z5 (151–190 bpm) to improve maximal oxygen uptake

entities.name: workouts.db
entities.context: SQLite database at Personal/Health/workouts.db storing workout logs with HR, duration, modality

entities.name: Workout Validation Script
entities.context: Python script that classifies runs by archetype and checks HR zone adherence with ±2 bpm tolerance

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database engine used for local workout data storage

entities.name: Treadmill Run
entities.context: One of the tracked primary modalities alongside outdoor Run

relationships.context: V structures running training around the 80/20 polarized model
V uses a 5-zone HR model calibrated to age 33 for workout classification
V logs workouts to a local SQLite database in Personal/Health/
Engine Building archetype targets Z2 (113–131 bpm) for aerobic base
20% Interval archetype targets Z4/Z5 (151–190 bpm) for VO2 max gains
Represents the 80% easy volume in the polarized model
